subject: Human Resources
title: Arbitration vs. Litigation
description: A fast breakdown of Arbitration vs. Litigation for builders.

hook.text: 60 million American workers have signed mandatory arbitration clauses—often buried in onboarding paperwork they never read.

buildup.text: Arbitration resolves disputes privately through a neutral arbitrator instead of a public courtroom and jury.

discovery.text: It's typically faster and cheaper than litigation. But critics say it favors employers—who choose and pay the arbitrators.

twist.text: Mandatory arbitration with class waivers prevents workers from joining forces—isolating claims too small to fight alone.

climax.text: The debate rages: efficiency versus access to justice. Some states are restricting mandatory arbitration for harassment claims.

punchline.text: Justice shouldn't depend on fine print nobody read.

quiz.question: Why do critics oppose mandatory arbitration?
options: It may favor employers and prevents class action lawsuits
It always results in higher awards for employees
